questions.question: In triangle ABC, the measure of angle A is 45° and the measure of angle B is 65°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in any triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 45° − 65° = 70°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one angle measuring 35°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: A right triangle has one 90° angle. The sum of all three angles is 180°, so the third angle is 180° − 90° − 35° = 55°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the vertex angle (the angle between the two equal sides) measures 50°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. If the vertex angle is 50°, then both base angles sum to 180° − 50° = 130°. Each base angle is 130° ÷ 2 = 65°.

questions.question: Two angles of a triangle measure 28° and 47°. The triangle is classified as acute, right, or obtuse?
questions.explanation: The third angle is 180° − 28° − 47° = 105°. Since one angle exceeds 90°, the triangle is obtuse.

questions.question: In triangle PQR, side PQ = 5 cm, side QR = 5 cm, and side PR = 6 cm. What type of triangle is this?
questions.explanation: A triangle is isosceles if at least two of its sides are equal. Since PQ = QR = 5 cm, triangle PQR is isosceles.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle measures 110°. What is the measure of the adjacent interior angle?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle and its adjacent interior angle form a linear pair, so they sum to 180°. The interior angle is 180° − 110° = 70°.
